title: Enrolment
metaDescription: Learn about enrolling at Rosehill College. Find all the information you need on application requirements, key dates, and the step-by-step process to join our vibrant school community. Start your journey with us today and become part of the Rosehill College family.

blocks.text: Rosehill College Enrolment Annual Enrolment We are excited to help you join our thriving learning community! Start your application today and take the first step towards becoming a valued member of the Rosehill College whānau. Please note: Enrolments for the following school year typically open at the start of Term 3 each year. For all enrolment enquiries, please email us at ✉️ enrolments@rosehillcollege.school.nz. In-zone Enrolment Complete your application online for a fast and straightforward process. If you do not have access to an electronic device, computers are available at our front office for you to complete your online enrolment. Our front office staff are also available to assist you with the process. Out-of-zone Enrolment All out-of-zone enrolment applicants must collect an Out-of-Zone Enrolment Pack from the school reception. Online applications for out-of-zone enrolments are not accepted and will not be processed. A non-refundable administration fee of $50 is payable when the completed out-of-zone application is submitted. This fee applies to all out-of-zone applications and is not refundable, regardless of the outcome of the ballot process. Privacy Commitment We are committed to safeguarding your personal information throughout the enrolment process. For more details, please review our Privacy Policy. Click here for quick navigation:  Re-enrolment  In-zone Enrolment  Out-of-zone Enrolment  Key Dates & Information  Course Information  Student Uniform  Prospectus Re-enrolment If your child has previously attended Rosehill College and you wish to re-enrol them, please contact us directly by emailing: 📧 enrolments@rosehillcollege.school.nz We are here to assist you with a smooth and efficient re-enrolment process. In-zone Enrolment Before You Start We strongly encourage you to complete the enrolment application online, ideally on a desktop computer or laptop for the best experience. Please note: submitting your application online does not guarantee automatic acceptance. All submissions will be reviewed, and you will receive confirmation via email once the process is complete. To get started, download the required forms (which can be filled out electronically using a Microsoft Edge browser or Adobe Acrobat for Drive). Be sure to save the completed forms, as they will be uploaded to the ‘Attachments’ section later in this application. Important Notes If you are unable to upload and submit the required forms and supporting documents online, you can complete the application with just the information and details. You may then bring the documents to the school office or email them separately to our enrolment email at enrolments@rosehillcollege.school.nz. Our front office staff are available to assist with scanning and entering your documents into our system. If you prefer to complete the enrolment process using paper forms, you can collect an enrolment pack from our front office. The office is open from 8am to 4pm, Monday to Friday. School Zone All students who live within the Rosehill College zone are entitled to enrol at the College. You can check if you are in zone for Rosehill College by entering your address at the following link: Click here to check for your school zone Enrolment Forms The following forms are COMPULSORY and must be submitted. Please download and fill out each form, ensuring they are completed in full. Note: All forms require a parent or guardian’s signature. It is essential that all required signatures are included — any unsigned forms will not be processed. The compulsory forms are as follows: Student Health Record In-zone Enrolment Questionnaire Education Outside the Classroom (EOTC) Consent Form 👉 Click here to download the checklist 👉 Click here to download the forms Please ensure all forms are signed and submitted to avoid any delays in the enrolment process. We also ask that you take a moment to read through Our Rosehill College Way, which outlines our school-wide rules and expectations. It is important that both you and your child/children familiarise yourselves with these guidelines before completing the enrolment. This helps ensure a smooth transition and supports a positive, respectful learning environment for all. Supporting Documents To facilitate the processing of enrolment application, we kindly request digital copies of the following documents, preferably in PDF format: Compulsory Documents Birth Certificate or Passport Student’s Visa: For students who are not New Zealand/Australian citizens. Legal or Guardianship documents: Any legal documents (e.g. Guardianship, Parenting Order, Custody Order, Protection Order), if applicable. Proof of Address: TWO different documents, each with the caregiver’s name, supply address or rating unit where the student lives, and with the most recent date. Tenancy Agreement or Council Rates bill Electricity bill or Water bill If you are unable to provide the required proof of address, you must complete a statutory declaration confirming your current residential address. This must be signed and witnessed by an authorised person (such as a Justice of the Peace). The declaration must include your full residential address and any other details required in the form. Failure to provide acceptable proof of address or a completed statutory declaration may result in a delay or refusal of enrolment. For further information or assistance, please contact the enrolment office. ‍👉 Click here to download the Statutory Declaration form Additional Supporting Documents Latest school report: From the student’s previous school File Naming and Format All uploaded documents should be in .pdf, .png, or .jpg format and clearly named. Please use the student’s last name, followed by first name, and then the document type. For example: Smith, John Enrolment Forms.pdf Confirmation of Enrolment If you are enrolling from within our designated zone, your child’s admission will be confirmed promptly upon the completion and verification of the required documents. Please be aware that this process may take some time, depending on the volume of applications. Once enrolment is confirmed, you will receive a confirmation email or letter. It is essential to ensure that we have been provided with your correct email address and phone number to avoid delays in communication. We remind all families that, under government legislation, schools are permitted to ask a student to leave immediately if it is found that they did not reside permanently within the Rosehill College zone at the time of enrolment. Start New Enrolment Application Start New Enrolment Application Upgrade in progress We are currently upgrading our online enrolment application to provide a better and more reliable experience for our whānau, students, and staff. Thank you! Your submission has been received! Oops! Something went wrong while submitting the form. Out-of-zone Enrolment Each year, the Rosehill College Board will determine the number of places likely to be available in the following year for the enrolment of students who live outside the home zone. The Board will publish this information on our school website. The notice will indicate how applications are to be made and will specify a date by which all applications must be received. Applications for enrolments will be processed in the following order of priority: First Priority: This priority is not applicable at this school because the school does not run a special programme approved by the Secretary for Education. Second Priority: will be given to applicants who are siblings of current students. Third Priority: will be given to applicants who are siblings of former students. Fourth Priority: will be given to any applicant who is a child of a former student of the school. Fifth Priority: will be given to any applicant who is either a child of an employee of the board of the school or a child of a member of the board of the school. Sixth Priority: will be given to all other applicants. If there are more applicants in the second, third, fourth, or fifth priority groups than there are available places, selection within the priority group will be determined by ballot, conducted in accordance with the instructions of the Secretary under Section 11G(1) of the Education Act 1989. Parents will be informed of the ballot date by notice in a daily or community newspaper circulating in the area served by the school. Applicants seeking second or third priority status may be required to give proof of a sibling relationship. Out-of-zone 2027 ‍Number of Placement The Rosehill College Board has carefully looked at the number of places we can offer for the upcoming school year. How many spaces are available depends on the number of in-zone enrolments we receive. The available places are listed below: Year 9 – 20 placements Year 10 – 10 placements Year 11 – 10 placements Year 12 – 10 placements Year 13 – 10 placements Application Fee Payment A non-refundable administration fee of $50.00 is payable upon submission of all out-of-zone enrolment applications, regardless of the ballot outcome. Payment Methods: EFTPOS (at the school office) Bank Transfer Bank Account Details: Account Name: Rosehill College Board‍Account Number: 12-3031-0837472-00 When making a bank transfer, please use the following payment references: Particular: OOZ2027Code: {Last Name}Reference: {First Name} Please ensure the payment references are entered exactly as shown to assist with the identification and processing of your application. Ballot Results The out-of-zone ballot is scheduled to take place on Wednesday, 2 September 2026. Parents and caregivers will be notified of the outcome via email shortly after the ballot has been conducted. If your child is offered a place, please confirm your acceptance within 14 days. Any unaccepted placements will be offered to students on the waiting list in the order their names were drawn. If you have not received your ballot result after the draw, please contact us for assistance. Please note: Additional ballots may only occur if the Board determines that further places become available during the school year. Out-of-zone application is now closed. Ballot Results The ballot for out-of-zone Year 2027 applications has now been completed. All applicants have been notified of their result by email. We warmly congratulate those who have been successful in the ballot, and we acknowledge those who were not offered a place this time. Successful applicants are required to confirm acceptance of their place within 14 days. Any places not accepted will be offered to students on the waiting list, in the order their names were drawn. Enrolment Key Dates & Information Enrolment Year 2027 Please note that the timeline for enrolment 2027 is provisional and subject to change. The dates outlined reflect our current planning and may be adjusted in accordance with Ministry of Education guidelines and internal scheduling updates. We encourage you to check our website regularly for the most up-to-date enrolment information. Dates Details Monday 20 July 2026 Start of Enrolment Year 2027 Tuesday 6 August 2025, 6pm Year 9 Pre-enrolment Parent Information Evening Saturday 8 August 2026 Rosehill College Open Day Wednesday 2 September 2026 Out-of-zone Application Closing Date Wednesday 9 September 2026 Ballot for out-of-zone placement, parents will be advised of a ballot outcome within 14 days Wednesday 10 December 2026 Year 8 Students Transition Days Course Information Detailed descriptions of each course Students Uniform Detailed information on uniform requirement and guidelines
Rosehill College Enrolment
Annual Enrolment
We are excited to help you join our thriving learning community! Start your application today and take the first step towards becoming a valued member of the Rosehill College whānau.
Please note: Enrolments for the following school year typically open at the start of Term 3 each year.
For all enrolment enquiries, please email us at ✉️ enrolments@rosehillcollege.school.nz.
In-zone Enrolment
Complete your application online for a fast and straightforward process.

If you do not have access to an electronic device, computers are available at our front office for you to complete your online enrolment. Our front office staff are also available to assist you with the process.
Out-of-zone Enrolment
All out-of-zone enrolment applicants must collect an Out-of-Zone Enrolment Pack from the school reception. Online applications for out-of-zone enrolments are not accepted and will not be processed.

A non-refundable administration fee of $50 is payable when the completed out-of-zone application is submitted. This fee applies to all out-of-zone applications and is not refundable, regardless of the outcome of the ballot process.
Privacy Commitment
We are committed to safeguarding your personal information throughout the enrolment process. For more details, please review our Privacy Policy.

Re-enrolment
If your child has previously attended Rosehill College and you wish to re-enrol them, please contact us directly by emailing:
📧 enrolments@rosehillcollege.school.nz
We are here to assist you with a smooth and efficient re-enrolment process.
In-zone Enrolment
We strongly encourage you to complete the enrolment application online, ideally on a desktop computer or laptop for the best experience.
Please note: submitting your application online does not guarantee automatic acceptance. All submissions will be reviewed, and you will receive confirmation via email once the process is complete.

To get started, download the required forms (which can be filled out electronically using a Microsoft Edge browser or Adobe Acrobat for Drive). Be sure to save the completed forms, as they will be uploaded to the ‘Attachments’ section later in this application.
Important Notes

If you are unable to upload and submit the required forms and supporting documents online, you can complete the application with just the information and details. You may then bring the documents to the school office or email them separately to our enrolment email at enrolments@rosehillcollege.school.nz. Our front office staff are available to assist with scanning and entering your documents into our system.
If you prefer to complete the enrolment process using paper forms, you can collect an enrolment pack from our front office. The office is open from 8am to 4pm, Monday to Friday.
All students who live within the Rosehill College zone are entitled to enrol at the College. You can check if you are in zone for Rosehill College by entering your address at the following link:
Click here to check for your school zone
The following forms are COMPULSORY and must be submitted. Please download and fill out each form, ensuring they are completed in full.
Note: All forms require a parent or guardian’s signature. It is essential that all required signatures are included — any unsigned forms will not be processed.
The compulsory forms are as follows:
Student Health Record
In-zone Enrolment Questionnaire
Education Outside the Classroom (EOTC) Consent Form
👉 Click here to download the checklist
👉 Click here to download the forms
Please ensure all forms are signed and submitted to avoid any delays in the enrolment process.

We also ask that you take a moment to read through Our Rosehill College Way, which outlines our school-wide rules and expectations. It is important that both you and your child/children familiarise yourselves with these guidelines before completing the enrolment. This helps ensure a smooth transition and supports a positive, respectful learning environment for all.
To facilitate the processing of enrolment application, we kindly request digital copies of the following documents, preferably in PDF format:
Compulsory Documents
Birth Certificate or Passport
Student’s Visa: For students who are not New Zealand/Australian citizens.
Legal or Guardianship documents: Any legal documents (e.g. Guardianship, Parenting Order, Custody Order, Protection Order), if applicable.

Proof of Address: TWO different documents, each with the caregiver’s name, supply address or rating unit where the student lives, and with the most recent date. Tenancy Agreement or Council Rates bill Electricity bill or Water bill
Tenancy Agreement or Council Rates bill
Electricity bill or Water bill

If you are unable to provide the required proof of address, you must complete a statutory declaration confirming your current residential address. This must be signed and witnessed by an authorised person (such as a Justice of the Peace). The declaration must include your full residential address and any other details required in the form. Failure to provide acceptable proof of address or a completed statutory declaration may result in a delay or refusal of enrolment. For further information or assistance, please contact the enrolment office.
‍👉 Click here to download the Statutory Declaration form
Additional Supporting Documents
Latest school report: From the student’s previous school
File Naming and Format

All uploaded documents should be in .pdf, .png, or .jpg format and clearly named. Please use the student’s last name, followed by first name, and then the document type. For example: Smith, John Enrolment Forms.pdf
Confirmation of Enrolment

If you are enrolling from within our designated zone, your child’s admission will be confirmed promptly upon the completion and verification of the required documents. Please be aware that this process may take some time, depending on the volume of applications. Once enrolment is confirmed, you will receive a confirmation email or letter.
It is essential to ensure that we have been provided with your correct email address and phone number to avoid delays in communication.

We remind all families that, under government legislation, schools are permitted to ask a student to leave immediately if it is found that they did not reside permanently within the Rosehill College zone at the time of enrolment.

Out-of-zone Enrolment

Each year, the Rosehill College Board will determine the number of places likely to be available in the following year for the enrolment of students who live outside the home zone. The Board will publish this information on our school website. The notice will indicate how applications are to be made and will specify a date by which all applications must be received.
Applications for enrolments will be processed in the following order of priority:
First Priority: This priority is not applicable at this school because the school does not run a special programme approved by the Secretary for Education.
Second Priority: will be given to applicants who are siblings of current students.
Third Priority: will be given to applicants who are siblings of former students.
Fourth Priority: will be given to any applicant who is a child of a former student of the school.
Fifth Priority: will be given to any applicant who is either a child of an employee of the board of the school or a child of a member of the board of the school.
Sixth Priority: will be given to all other applicants.

If there are more applicants in the second, third, fourth, or fifth priority groups than there are available places, selection within the priority group will be determined by ballot, conducted in accordance with the instructions of the Secretary under Section 11G(1) of the Education Act 1989. Parents will be informed of the ballot date by notice in a daily or community newspaper circulating in the area served by the school.
Applicants seeking second or third priority status may be required to give proof of a sibling relationship.
Out-of-zone 2027
‍Number of Placement

The Rosehill College Board has carefully looked at the number of places we can offer for the upcoming school year. How many spaces are available depends on the number of in-zone enrolments we receive. The available places are listed below:
Year 9 – 20 placements
Year 10 – 10 placements
Year 11 – 10 placements
Year 12 – 10 placements
Year 13 – 10 placements
Application Fee Payment
A non-refundable administration fee of $50.00 is payable upon submission of all out-of-zone enrolment applications, regardless of the ballot outcome.
Payment Methods:
EFTPOS (at the school office)
Bank Transfer
Bank Account Details:
Account Name: Rosehill College Board‍Account Number: 12-3031-0837472-00
When making a bank transfer, please use the following payment references:
Particular: OOZ2027Code: {Last Name}Reference: {First Name}
Please ensure the payment references are entered exactly as shown to assist with the identification and processing of your application.
Ballot Results
The out-of-zone ballot is scheduled to take place on Wednesday, 2 September 2026. Parents and caregivers will be notified of the outcome via email shortly after the ballot has been conducted.
If your child is offered a place, please confirm your acceptance within 14 days. Any unaccepted placements will be offered to students on the waiting list in the order their names were drawn.
If you have not received your ballot result after the draw, please contact us for assistance.
Please note: Additional ballots may only occur if the Board determines that further places become available during the school year.
Out-of-zone application is now closed.
The ballot for out-of-zone Year 2027 applications has now been completed. All applicants have been notified of their result by email.
We warmly congratulate those who have been successful in the ballot, and we acknowledge those who were not offered a place this time.
Successful applicants are required to confirm acceptance of their place within 14 days. Any places not accepted will be offered to students on the waiting list, in the order their names were drawn.
Enrolment Key Dates & Information
Enrolment Year 2027

Please note that the timeline for enrolment 2027 is provisional and subject to change. The dates outlined reflect our current planning and may be adjusted in accordance with Ministry of Education guidelines and internal scheduling updates. We encourage you to check our website regularly for the most up-to-date enrolment information.
